prerequisites: A Planhat account with access to the App Center and the Private Apps (Service Accounts) feature.
Permission to manage Private Apps: the 'Serviceaccount' data model permission must be enabled for your user or role.
An API Access Token generated from a Private App in Planhat settings (this is the value for the connector's required api_token field).

authMethods.name: API Access Token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Planhat authenticates API requests with an API Access Token sent in the Authorization header as 'Bearer {token}'. Generate the token from a Private App in Planhat. Paste it into the connector's api_token field. The token is shown only once at creation, so copy it immediately; if lost you must generate a new one.

setupSteps.title: Open the App Center and create a new app
setupSteps.body: In Planhat, open the App Center from the Global Tools and click '+ New app'. You need the 'Serviceaccount' data model permission to view and manage Private Apps.

setupSteps.title: Create a Private App (Service Account)
setupSteps.body: In the modal that appears, click '+ Private app'. Give the app a descriptive name and set its permissions (start from scratch, copy from an existing Private App, or copy from a Role), then click 'Create'.

setupSteps.title: Generate and copy the API Access Token
setupSteps.body: In the Private App configuration modal, on the Info tab, click 'Generate new token'. The token is displayed only once, so copy it and store it securely. It does not expire and can later be revoked by disabling the Private App or removing the token.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the generated token into the connector's required api_token field. The connector sends it to the Planhat API as 'Authorization: Bearer {api_token}'.

sources.title: How to set up Private Apps (Service Accounts) and API Access Tokens in Planhat
